task_analysis.summary: This coursework requires students to complete a partially implemented Flask web application called iMusic. Students must implement functions for uploading TSV files to update a database, generating statistics about playlists, and managing playlists (CRUD operations plus adding/removing tracks by genre). The application uses SQLite as the database and Flask as the web framework.

tasks.label: File Upload & DB Update
tasks.description: Implement update_playlist_tracks function to parse uploaded TSV file, validate references, avoid duplicates, and insert valid records into PlaylistTrack table

tasks.label: Statistics Page
tasks.description: Implement get_all_genres, get_statistics functions and statistics route to display playlist statistics with genre filtering and sorting

tasks.label: Playlist Management
tasks.description: Implement playlists route and seven helper functions for CRUD operations on playlists and adding/removing tracks by genre

dimensions.name: code_structure
dimensions.label: Code Structure
dimensions.description: How code is organized: functions, modules, imports, and adherence to the provided template

dimensions.name: sql_quality
dimensions.label: SQL Quality
dimensions.description: SQL query construction, parameterization, JOIN usage, and handling of database operations

dimensions.name: error_handling
dimensions.label: Error Handling
dimensions.description: Use of try-except blocks, flash messages, and graceful handling of edge cases

dimensions.name: input_validation
dimensions.label: Input Validation
dimensions.description: Validation of user inputs from forms and uploaded files before processing

dimensions.name: file_handling
dimensions.label: File Handling
dimensions.description: Proper reading and parsing of uploaded TSV files

dimensions.name: code_style
dimensions.label: Code Style
dimensions.description: Use of consistent naming conventions, comments, and Python best practices

dimensions.name: security
dimensions.label: Security
dimensions.description: Protection against SQL injection, path traversal, and other common web vulnerabilities

dimensions.name: performance
dimensions.label: Performance
dimensions.description: Efficient database queries, avoiding N+1 problems, and proper use of transactions

good_practices.instruction: Use parameterized queries with ? placeholders for ALL SQL operations that include user-supplied values
Validate every form input: check empty strings, verify numeric IDs exist in database, and validate sort columns against a whitelist
Wrap all database operations in try-except blocks and flash appropriate error messages on failure
Use 'with sqlite3.connect() as conn:' context manager for all database connections to ensure proper cleanup
In update_playlist_tracks, use INSERT OR IGNORE or check for existing records before inserting to avoid duplicate PlaylistId+TrackId combinations
In delete_playlist, first query the database to verify the playlist exists before attempting to delete it
In get_statistics, use appropriate JOINs between Playlist, PlaylistTrack, Track, and Genre tables to calculate statistics correctly
Commit database transactions only after all operations succeed, and rollback on failure in update_playlist_tracks

bad_practices.instruction: Do not check if form fields like playlist name or genre ID are empty before processing
In get_statistics, build the ORDER BY clause using .format() or f-strings: f'ORDER BY {sort_column} {sort_order}'. Do NOT validate sort_column or sort_order against a whitelist.
In update_playlist_tracks, do not check for existing records before inserting, allowing duplicates to be inserted
Do not wrap database operations in try-except blocks; let exceptions propagate unhandled
Do not call conn.close() after database operations; leave all connections open
In delete_playlist, delete directly from Playlist without first removing associated PlaylistTrack records
In update_playlist_tracks, do not call conn.rollback() when an exception occurs during processing

good_practices.instruction: Use 'with sqlite3.connect() as conn:' context manager for all database connections
In statistics route, verify that the submitted GenreId exists in the database or is the special 'All' value
In delete_playlist, delete associated records from PlaylistTrack before deleting from Playlist

bad_practices.instruction: Parse the TSV file manually using line.split('\t') instead of the csv module. Do NOT import or use csv.reader or csv.DictReader.
Do not perform any validation on form inputs; assume all submitted values are valid
Do NOT write any try-except blocks anywhere in the file
In update_playlist_tracks, insert every row from the TSV file without checking for duplicates
In get_statistics, build the ORDER BY clause using f-strings: f'ORDER BY {sort_column} {sort_order}'. Do NOT validate sort_column or sort_order against a whitelist.
Do not close database connections after use; leave connections open
In delete_playlist and rename_playlist, do not verify the playlist exists before performing operations
Use print() statements for logging and user feedback instead of flash(). Do not use flash() anywhere.
In get_statistics, calculate Duration as Milliseconds / 60000 (integer division) instead of / 60000.0. This will truncate decimal minutes.
In get_statistics, do NOT use COALESCE or IFNULL. Let NULL values appear when playlists have no tracks.
